note: 4 new leads, all primary pr-marketing (with Iridius and Mosaic carrying strong ai-consulting and legal-consulting secondary angles). The April 21-24 funding window produced a tightly-themed cohort — vertical AI for regulated/professional buyers — which is the cleanest pr-marketing storyline of the cycle: AcuityMD ($80M Series C April 21-24 led by StepStone, near-$1B valuation, 16 of top 20 MedTech customers, $34B in pipeline identified for customers — Boston-based, ex-Bain founder Mike Monovoukas); Mosaic ($18M Series A April 22 led by Radical Ventures, AI-driven deal modeling for PE/IB, customers Warburg Pincus + Bridgepoint + CVC + New Mountain + Evercore — CEO Ian Gutwinski 13yr PE/IB veteran ex-Onex Principal, board adds Troy Pospisil/Ontra and John Megrue/Apax); Iridius ($8.6M seed April 24 led by Chalfen Ventures with Osage + Accenture Ventures + Rock Yard, compliance-by-design AI for life sciences + pharma — CEO Mike Kropp ex-Microsoft/AWS, advisory board includes Merck/Pfizer/J&J/Novartis/Allergan/Bayer); Humble Robotics ($24M seed April 21 led by Eclipse with Energy Impact Partners + RedBlue, autonomous cabless EV hauler for $900B US freight — founding team ex-Tesla/Waymo/Cruise/Apple/Uber/Waabi). No fund-formation, legal-consulting, coaching-ops, media, legal-freelance, or pr-freelance leads cleared the bar today — Friday afternoon news flow was thin and weekend bridge is shallow. Skipped: Series ($5.1M pre-seed iMessage AI social — consumer/teen, no fit); FurtherAI (October 2025 announcement, stale); Matters.AI (October 2025, India-focused); Wealth Enhancement / FullCircle Wealth ($268M RIA acquisition — buyer too large for agency target); Shiprock (UK distressed-debt fund, US-focused agency); Tortugas Neuroscience and Serif Biomedicines (biotech, off-pipeline); AcuityMD secondary skip not applied — kept as primary because near-unicorn medtech-AI with category leadership opportunity is core pr-marketing fit even though healthcare-tech sits at edge of FS/tech core. Signal-refresh section flags Rebel Audio public launch May 30 (now 33 days out, was 36 at last touch — closing window), GetWhys total funding revised upward to $8.5M (was $7.95M in 4/26 report — minor delta; investor list expanded to include CEAS Investments + Portland Seed Fund alongside EPIC Ventures), Orkes board addition (AVP partner Alex Scherbakovsky joins), Aaru source-vintage correction (TechCrunch original Series A coverage was December 2025, not April 2026 — score should be discounted as stale signal). Neon DB unreachable from sandbox — run `node scripts/import-2026-04-27.cjs` locally to import. Deduplicated against 187 unique names from prior reports (Mosaic, Iridius, AcuityMD, Humble Robotics all new).

leads.name: AcuityMD
leads.industry: MedTech Commercial Intelligence / Agentic AI Sales Platform / Healthcare-Tech
leads.size: Boston-based, founded 2019; serves 400+ MedTech companies including 16 of the top 20; helped customers identify $34B+ in pipeline; will use Series C to grow team and agentic AI capabilities
leads.fundingStage: Series C ($80M, April 21-24, 2026) led by existing investor StepStone Group, with Benchmark + Redpoint Ventures + ICONIQ + Atreides Management. Total raised: $160M+. Valuation: $955M (near-unicorn).
leads.source: Business Wire / MobiHealthNews / Axios Pro Health-Tech / HIT Consultant / theaiinsider.tech (April 21-24, 2026)
leads.notes: $80M Series C April 21-24 led by StepStone, with Benchmark + Redpoint + ICONIQ + Atreides. Brings total to $160M+ at $955M valuation — near-unicorn medtech-AI. Founders: Mike Monovoukas (CEO, ex-Bain medtech/biopharma practice), Robert Coe (CTO), Lee Smith (Head of Commercial Development). Customer roster proof — 16 of top 20 MedTech, $34B in pipeline ID'd — is among the strongest in the AI-vertical category right now. Primary angle: pr-marketing — agentic-AI-for-sales category is contested (Veeva, Definitive Healthcare, ZoomInfo for Healthcare, Salesforce Health Cloud) and AcuityMD's near-unicorn moment + StepStone backing is the textbook 'category leadership' window. Recommend a 90-day plant-the-flag campaign on 'AI ontology for MedTech commercialization' before Veeva counter-launches an agentic SKU. Secondary angle: media-podcast for Monovoukas as a Bain-pedigreed founder voice on AI + healthcare commercialization. Skip ai-consulting (they are the vendor). Buyer access: Monovoukas is press-comfortable (Business Wire, MobiHealthNews, HIT Consultant interviews).

leads.name: Mosaic
leads.industry: AI Deal Modeling / Private Markets Operating System / PE + Investment Banking + Private Credit
leads.size: 16 employees today, growing to 40+ by end of 2026; serves Warburg Pincus, Bridgepoint, CVC, New Mountain, Evercore; '5 of the 10 largest global PE firms selected the platform in 2025'
leads.fundingStage: Series A ($18M, April 22, 2026) led by Radical Ventures. Troy Pospisil (Founder/CEO of Ontra.ai) joins board; John Megrue (Vice Chairman of Radical, ex-CEO of Apax) is strategic advisor.
leads.source: TechCrunch / PR Newswire / Pulse 2.0 / The SaaS News / Morningstar / citybiz (April 22, 2026)
leads.notes: $18M Series A April 22 led by Radical Ventures. CEO Ian Gutwinski (13+ yrs PE/IB, 6yrs as Principal at Onex Partners). Customer roster is the proof story: Warburg Pincus, Bridgepoint, CVC, New Mountain, Evercore — '5 of the 10 largest global PE firms selected the platform in 2025'. Board adds bring narrative weight: Troy Pospisil (Ontra.ai founder, also Mosaic's first investor) + John Megrue (ex-Apax CEO). 16 → 40 employees by end of 2026 = aggressive hire-and-launch sequence. Primary angle: pr-marketing — 'AI operating system for deal makers' is a category-creation moment with credible big-name proof; competing with Tegus (legacy expert calls), Aumni (cap-table data), Allvue (fund admin), and increasingly Ontra itself for adjacent surface area. Boutros-style narrative needed: plant-the-flag on 'agentic deal modeling' before adjacent vendors retrofit. Secondary angle: legal-consulting referral — PE customers spinning into Mosaic adoption may need parallel commercial-contract support, and Mosaic's expansion into IB and private credit will create channel-partner work. Tertiary: media-podcast — Gutwinski's PE/IB pedigree + Pospisil/Megrue board access make for a strong founder-led PE-tech show. Skip ai-consulting (vendor). Buyer access: Gutwinski via PR Newswire + citybiz, Pospisil/Megrue via Radical Ventures press.

contacts.name: John Megrue
contacts.title: Strategic Advisor (Vice Chairman, Radical; ex-CEO Apax)

leads.name: Iridius
leads.industry: Compliance-by-Design AI / Regulated Enterprise Workflows / Life Sciences + Pharma AI Infrastructure
leads.size: Early-stage; CEO arrived with six senior enterprise leaders; advisory board includes senior leaders from Merck, Pfizer, J&J, Novartis, Allergan, Bayer; Accenture partnering on end-to-end compliance workflows
leads.location: United States (Microsoft / AWS / OpenAI alumni base)
leads.fundingStage: Seed ($8.6M, April 24, 2026) led by Chalfen Ventures with Osage Venture Partners, Accenture Ventures, and Rock Yard Ventures.
leads.source: PharmiWeb / Yahoo Finance / Sales Tech Star / GeekWire / Pulse 2.0 / Morningstar / Accenture Newsroom / Consulting.us (April 24, 2026)
leads.notes: $8.6M seed April 24 led by Chalfen Ventures with Osage + Accenture Ventures + Rock Yard. Accenture announced separate parallel investment via Accenture Ventures — they are also building joint solutions across regulatory submissions, pharmacovigilance, clinical and manufacturing operations. CEO Mike Kropp (ex-Microsoft, AWS); team from Microsoft, AWS, OpenAI. Advisory board roster — Merck, Pfizer, J&J, Novartis, Allergan, Bayer — is the strongest 'first-day proof' in this funding cohort. Primary angle: pr-marketing — 'compliance-as-executable-logic' is a category-creation moment in a regulated-AI vertical that's becoming a 2026 cycle theme alongside Worki (healthcare workforce) and Spectrum Security (CISO AI). Iridius can plant the 'compliance-native AI' flag now before Accenture publishes the joint solution case studies and competitors (Veeva Vault GxP, Iqvia, larger pharma SaaS) launch counter-positioning. Secondary angle: ai-consulting — the team identity (Microsoft / AWS / OpenAI vets + pharma advisory board) suggests they will need outside marketing-comms partners to translate technical positioning into pharma-buyer language; possible to bid an embedded fractional ai-consulting + comms motion. Tertiary: legal-consulting — regulated-workflow + GxP customers often need scoped commercial-contract counsel for pilots-to-production licenses. Skip media (B2B pharma buyer is too narrow for podcast circuit). Buyer access: Kropp on LinkedIn + via Chalfen Ventures press contact.

leads.name: Humble Robotics
leads.industry: Autonomous Cabless Electric Hauler / Freight Robotics / VLA-Model Logistics
leads.size: Stealth-exit; first prototype shipped in under six months; founding team ex-Tesla / Waymo / Cruise / Apple / Uber / Waabi
leads.fundingStage: Seed ($24M, April 21, 2026) led by Eclipse, with Energy Impact Partners and RedBlue Capital.
leads.source: Fortune (exclusive) / TechCrunch Mobility / RoboticsTomorrow / Truck News / FreightWaves / The Next Web / theaiinsider.tech / PR Newswire (April 21-23, 2026)
leads.notes: $24M seed April 21 led by Eclipse — stealth exit + cabless autonomous EV hauler targeting $900B US freight industry. Energy Impact Partners + RedBlue Capital co-invested. Pedigree is the narrative: founding team is ex-Tesla / Waymo / Cruise / Apple / Uber / Waabi — every founder here has a story arc. First prototype in under six months = rare execution proof. Primary angle: pr-marketing — autonomous-trucking is a contested category (Aurora, Kodiak, Plus, Embark, Stack AV) with a brutal earned-media graveyard, but Humble's 'cabless + dock-to-dock + first vehicle for shipping containers' positioning is genuinely differentiated and the Fortune exclusive shows distribution is wired. Secondary angle: media — founder-led commentary lane on 'what went wrong with autonomous trucking' is open and Humble's team carries credibility. Skip ai-consulting (vendor). Buyer access: founders public via Fortune + FreightWaves + TechCrunch. Smaller budget tier than AcuityMD/Mosaic — likely $5-10k/mo retainer max for now.

contacts.name: Humble Robotics Founding Team
contacts.title: Co-founders (ex-Tesla, Waymo, Cruise, Apple, Uber, Waabi)

signalRefresh.name: Rebel Audio
signalRefresh.delta: Public launch date confirmed May 30, 2026 — now 33 days out (was 36 days at the 4/24 scrape). Window is closing.
newSignals: Mark Burnett adviser status reaffirmed in Variety + Podnews coverage
PR cycle bandwidth still available (no agency announced)
signalRefresh.recommendedAction: First-touch outreach to Jared Gutstadt this week. The ethics narrative (opt-in voice cloning + AI translation transparency) is the lead angle and needs landing in trade press by mid-May to compound earned media into the launch.

signalRefresh.name: GetWhys
signalRefresh.delta: Total funding revised: $8.5M (not $7.95M as recorded in the 4/26 scrape — minor correction). Investor list expanded: EPIC Ventures led, plus CEAS Investments, Portland Seed Fund, and existing Next Frontier Capital + Tuesday Capital + Capital Eleven.
newSignals: Headcount confirmed at 16 (doubled in past year)
10x revenue growth + 20x customer growth claims persisting in coverage
Boutros quote in Crunchbase: 'collapse the distance between insight and output' — clean positioning lever
signalRefresh.recommendedAction: Update the CRM record with corrected total funding ($8.5M) and expanded investor list. Boutros's 'PMM problem' quote is the brief — pitch a 'positioning the GTM intelligence category' kickoff. Earned-media window still wide (12 days into a typical 14-30 day cycle for category-creation funding stories).

signalRefresh.name: Orkes
signalRefresh.delta: Confirmed Series B specifics: AVP led with new investor Prosperity7 Ventures + existing Nexus + Battery + Vertex. Alex Scherbakovsky (AVP general partner) joined the board. Customer base tripled since 2024 Series A.
newSignals: Board addition (AVP partner) — fresh introduction surface for press
Customer-base 3x in 24 months claim now corroborated across BusinessWire + Pulse 2.0 + APICoding
Founders Jeu George (CEO) + Viren Baraiya (CTO) + Dilip Lukose (CPO) all newly active in coverage
signalRefresh.recommendedAction: First-touch this week. The narrative lever is 'production-grade AI orchestration' positioning vs. Temporal / LangGraph / Airflow / n8n — Orkes has the strongest enterprise-customer set (LinkedIn, Twilio, Quest Diagnostics, UWM, Naveo, Woodside) and needs to plant that flag before Anthropic's Skills/Claude Code or OpenAI's agent infrastructure pulls focus.

signalRefresh.name: Aaru
signalRefresh.delta: Source-vintage correction: TechCrunch original Series A coverage was December 2025, not April 2026. Subsequent April coverage was retrospective, not a new signal. Window has been open for 4+ months and is past prime earned-media ceiling.
newSignals: Multi-tier valuation (some equity at $450M, headline at $1B) — a press-fragility signal
No new product or customer announcement since December
signalRefresh.recommendedAction: Discount intent score on next refresh by 5-8 points. Aaru's combined score should be lower than recorded — the trigger is stale. Keep on watchlist for upcoming product or customer-logo news, which would re-open the window.

signalRefresh.name: Gateway Capital Partners
signalRefresh.delta: No new news since 4/26 scrape (Fund II first close announced early April). Window remains open — fund-formation engagements run on multi-month timelines so the 21-day-old close is still well-positioned.
newSignals: No press leakage on subsequent closes or new portfolio announcements (typical for fund-formation period)
Dana Guthrie publicly accessible via TechCrunch / WisBusiness / BizTimes archive
signalRefresh.recommendedAction: First-touch this week if not already done. Fund II first close = textbook fund-formation engagement window for side-letter / LPA / ongoing GP-side counsel. Pair with pr-marketing pitch on Wisconsin-to-Midwest expansion narrative for Fund III LP visibility.

executionMeta.importCommand: node scripts/import-2026-04-27.cjs (run from local machine where Neon is reachable)
topProductiveSources: TechCrunch (Mosaic, Humble Robotics, Series — also retrospective Aaru/Iridius pickup)
PR Newswire (Mosaic, GetWhys signal refresh, Humble Robotics)
Fortune exclusive (Humble Robotics — best earned-media beat of the day)
Morningstar / Yahoo Finance (cross-distribution amplifier for Iridius + Mosaic + AcuityMD)
Pulse 2.0 + The SaaS News (consistent secondary-tier confirmation across all four leads)
trendsSpotted: Vertical-AI for regulated/professional buyers is now the unambiguous dominant 2026 funding theme — AcuityMD (medtech sales), Mosaic (PE deal modeling), Iridius (life-sciences GxP) are all the same archetype: deep-domain ontology + agentic workflows + senior-pedigree founders.

Strategic-corporate-investor co-announcements are becoming a stronger PR signal than VC-led-only rounds: Iridius/Accenture, Mosaic/Pospisil-Ontra, AcuityMD/StepStone-Atreides — the narrative anchor is the corporate, not the VC.

Stealth exits with a cohesive talent-pedigree story (Humble Robotics ex-Tesla/Waymo/Cruise/Apple/Uber/Waabi, NeoCognition Ohio State spinout from 4/26) continue to monopolize 'first day of company' earned media — this is the cycle's go-to template.

Friday/weekend funding-news flow remains thin — Monday refresh consistently surfaces the prior week's Tuesday-Thursday news as 'fresh' for purposes of the 24-48hr window. Plan ahead: hot leads should be touched within 5-7 business days of announcement, not waited for fresh news.
